summary: Lists are mutable, ordered sequences. Indexing is O(1). `.append()` is O(1) amortized. In-place methods return `None`.
oneLineSummary: Lists are mutable ordered dynamic arrays of object references.
keyTakeaways: sorted() returns new list; .sort() mutates in-place.
Slicing lst[a:b] creates a shallow copy.

memoryTricks.trick: Methods that modify in-place return None (Silent Modifiers).

commonErrors.cause: Assigning result of .sort() or .append() to variable
commonErrors.fix: Do not assign .sort()/.append() calls to variables

nextTopics.title: Tuple Immutability
nextTopics.whyNext: Learn fixed immutable sequences using tuples.
